figures.caption: Fig. 5: Comparison of DA performance using RKHS-MMD and without DA methods

Fig. 1: An illustration of DA in classification. (a) Initial classification shows the mismatch between source and target domains, resulting in poor generalization. (b) Through DA, the feature distributions of the two domains are aligned. (c) Final classification demonstrates improved alignment and accurate predictions on the target domain.
Fig. 2: DA architecture using EfficientNetV2, combining classification and MMD losses to predict target labels from unannotated data.
